title: System Synchronization

content.question: Which two systems are fighting each other instead of working together?
sop: Map every major system in your business (content, sales, delivery, admin) and how they hand off to each other.
Identify where timing or output from one system creates a bottleneck or conflict in another.
Rate how many of your systems are actually Aligned versus fighting each other (see calculation, page 4)
Adjust timing, sequencing, or ownership so the misaligned systems hand off cleanly instead of colliding.
Re-map monthly, new systems you add will need to be synced in too.
questions: Which two systems are fighting each other instead of working together?
Where does finishing one part of your process actively create a backlog somewhere else?
If you mapped your business as gears, which one is turning against the others instead of with them?
content.equation_example: Aligned Systems = 3, Total Systems = 5. Efficiency = 3 ÷ 5 = 60%. Higher means more of your systems reinforce rather than fight each other. Lower means most of your systems are working against each other, quietly wasting the output each one produces.
content.closing_statement: Unsynced systems cancel out each other's progress.

caption:
Principle Nº 141: System Synchronization

Systems must work together.

Ask yourself: Which two systems are fighting each other instead of working together?
